title: Majestas Populi.
content:
Majesty of the nature of man! In crowds shall I seek thee?
    'Tis with only a few that thou hast made thine abode.
    Only a few ever count; the rest are but blanks of no value,
    And the prizes are hid 'neath the vain stir that they make.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Friedrich Schiller delivers a powerful performance in "Majestas Populi."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
